_meta.source_report: inputs/deepsearch/Immature Neuron Cell Types and Development Across Brain Regions.pdf

issues.description: Node primarily represents type-3 neuroblasts (DCX+ only) but quotes also describe type-2a (Nestin+/Sox2+) and type-2b (Nestin+/DCX+) upstream stages. Separate sub-nodes may be warranted.
No dedicated UBERON term for subgranular zone (SGZ); UBERON:0001885 (dentate gyrus) used as container.

Glutamatergic identity is anticipatory for proliferating type-3 neuroblasts — they are committed to the lineage (via Pax6>Ngn2>Tbr2>NeuroD>Tbr1 cascade) but not yet functionally glutamatergic at this stage.
No direct ephys characterisation of the proliferating type-3 stage. Ephys quotes relate to downstream postmitotic stages.
No neuropeptide information for DG type-3 neuroblasts in the report.

gaps: species: mouse, rat, human referenced but not confirmed
anatomical_location: SGZ lacks dedicated UBERON term

issues.description: Calbindin absence is inferred from the stage-6 marker profile contrast (Calbindin emerges later); no quote directly asserts Calbindin-negative for stage 5.
Inner granule cell layer lacks a dedicated UBERON term; UBERON:0001885 (dentate gyrus) used as container.
Circuitry/connectivity described (glutamatergic input from EC, mossy fiber output to CA3) but no direct intrinsic electrophysiological characterisation (patch-clamp) of the stage-5 immature neuron.
No neuropeptide information in the report for DG stage-5 immature granule neurons.

gaps: CL term: no existing CL match — proposed new term CL:9900002 (status DRAFT)
negative_markers: direct Calbindin-negative claim at stage 5 would strengthen provenance
species: mouse, rat, human referenced but not confirmed

issues.description: DCX/Nestin/PSA-NCAM downregulation at stage 6 is inferred from the developmental progression described by Micheli et al.; not explicitly stated as 'negative' in any quote.
Dendritic arbor / mossy fiber morphology for the mature stage is not quantitatively described in the report's quotes; the description is largely terminal-point and connectivity-focused.
Connectivity (EC input, CA3 output) and Tis21 decrease are captured but no direct intrinsic/patch-clamp data for mature DG granule cells in these quotes.
No neuropeptide information in the report for mature DG granule cells.

gaps: electrophysiology_class: no direct intrinsic recordings captured
species: mouse, rat, human referenced but not confirmed

issues.description: Receptor absence (NMDA-R, glucocorticoid-R) and activity marker absence (c-Fos, Arc) are strictly ephys/functional rather than lineage markers. Encoded here as negative_markers for schema fit but curator review recommended.

Glutamatergic identity applies only to the subpopulation that resumes maturation (TBR1+/CaMKII+). The bulk of layer II immature neurons remain non-integrated and lack a mature neurotransmitter phenotype.
Proposed type spans two main morphological subtypes (tangled cells, complex cells) plus transitional forms. May warrant separate sub-nodes.
No neuropeptides characterised in the report for piriform cortex layer II immature neurons.
No explicit colocated types reported; astroglial lamellae ensheathment is mentioned but cells are generally non-integrated.

gaps: species: multiple species (mouse, rat, guinea pig) but not confirmed
cross-species validity: degu finding (Groen et al., 2021) shows sparse DCX+ cells in degu piriform cortex — species-specific caveat

issues.description: The node spans three distinct anatomical compartments (SVZ origin, RMS migration path, OB destination). No dedicated UBERON term for RMS; SVZ (UBERON:0002264) used as closest container but understates the geographical span.
GABAergic identity refers to the terminal interneuron fate after maturation; migrating type A neuroblasts are not yet functionally GABAergic.
Timing of synaptic integration (21 days) is captured, but intrinsic electrophysiological properties of the immature neuroblast stage are not characterised in the report.
No neuropeptides characterised in the report for OB neuroblasts / immature interneurons.

gaps: electrophysiology_class: intrinsic properties of immature stage not directly recorded
species: mouse/rat referenced but not confirmed
anatomical_location: RMS has no dedicated UBERON term

issues.description: Only DCX and BrdU (birth-dating label, not a molecular marker) are reported. No lineage-specific or fate-specifying markers are characterised.
No negative markers reported for striatal immature neurons.
NT type assigned by analogy with SVZ-derived OB interneurons (GABAergic), but no quote directly characterises NT type of striatal immature neurons.
Only migration-consistent observation; no detailed dendritic/axonal or developmental-stage morphology.
No electrophysiological characterisation reported; adult striatal neurogenesis is quantitatively limited and controversial.
No neuropeptide information available.
No colocated types reported.

gaps: species: mouse reference; others not discussed

issues.description: Only DCX reported as a defining marker; no lineage-defining or fate-specifying markers available.
NT type unspecified; no quote addresses neurotransmitter identity of cortical layer II immature neurons.
Only anatomical distribution reported; no detailed morphological maturation stages.
No electrophysiological characterisation reported; functional significance of cortical immature neurons remains controversial and species-dependent.
No neuropeptide information available.
No colocated types reported.

gaps: species: cross-species contradictions (mouse/rat vs degu/guinea pig)

global_issues.description: Dayer et al. (2005) (PMID:15684031) reported no DCX+ cells in the rodent neocortex — contradicting Groen et al. (2021) (PMID:33994960) who reported widespread DCX+ cells in the degu neocortex, and Yang et al. (2015) (PMID:26321922) in guinea pig. This is a species-dependent contradiction affecting cx_immature_neuron.

Distinction between 'neurogenesis-derived immature neuron' (SGZ/SVZ lineages: dg_neuroblast, dg_immature_granule_neuron, ob_neuroblast, str_immature_neuron) and 'prenatally-generated lifelong immature neuron' (pir_immature_neuron, cx_immature_neuron) spans very different biology. CL:4042028/4042029 cover only the latter; CL:0000031 is used for the former; dg_mature_granule_neuron has an EXACT CL:2000089 mapping; dg_immature_granule_neuron has no existing CL term and is proposed as new (CL:9900002).

Multiple proposed nodes conflate developmental stages or morphological subtypes that may merit separate sub-nodes (dg_neuroblast: type-2a/2b/3; ob_neuroblast: SVZ type A → RMS → OB granule/periglomerular; pir_immature_neuron: tangled/complex cells). The DG lineage has already been split into three stage-specific nodes (dg_neuroblast, dg_immature_granule_neuron, dg_mature_granule_neuron).
Neuropeptide information is absent across all 7 nodes in the report. This is a systematic gap of the ASTA deep research source rather than a per-node curation deficit.

round2_targets.action: Search primary literature for intrinsic electrophysiological properties of proliferating type-3 progenitors (e.g., Espósito et al., 2005; Overstreet-Wadiche/Westbrook reviews).
Decide whether to split into separate type-2a and type-2b sub-nodes upstream of the current type-3-focused node.
Submit proposed CL term CL:9900002 (dentate gyrus immature granule neuron) to CL editors; fill the gap between CL:0000031 (neuroblast) and CL:2000089 (mature granule cell).

Locate patch-clamp / intrinsic recordings of stage-5 immature granule neurons (DCX+/NeuN+) — Espósito et al. 2005 and subsequent work tracks input resistance, firing patterns across maturation timepoints.
Source a direct-statement quote confirming Calbindin-negative status at stage 5 (currently inferred from downstream stage-6 marker emergence).
Source direct-statement quotes confirming DCX/Nestin/PSA-NCAM downregulation at stage 6 (currently inferred from lineage progression).
Add direct intrinsic electrophysiology characterisation quotes for mature DG granule cells (e.g., from classical hippocampal ephys literature).
Decide whether to split into 'tangled cell' vs 'complex cell' sub-nodes; consult Gómez-Climent et al., 2008 and Rotheneichner et al., 2018.
Search primary literature for neuropeptide expression in piriform cortex layer II immature neurons.
Search primary literature for neuropeptide expression in migrating Type A neuroblasts and immature OB interneurons.
Decide whether to split into SVZ Type A, RMS migrating, and OB immature interneuron (granule vs periglomerular) sub-nodes.
Broader literature search needed — current support is a single study (Dayer et al., 2005). Check for more recent reports or dismissals of adult striatal neurogenesis.
Determine NT type empirically or decide node should be removed if unsupported by primary literature.
Locate any morphological characterisation; otherwise annotate node as minimally-characterised and flag for curator review.
Search for additional lineage/fate markers beyond DCX (e.g. NeuroD, TBR1, layer-specific markers) in cortical layer II immature neurons.
Determine NT type — pir_immature_neuron is glutamatergic upon maturation; check whether cortical cINs follow the same fate in mouse/degu/guinea pig.
Resolve cross-species discrepancy: Dayer et al. (2005) found no neocortical DCX+ in mouse; degu and guinea pig reports are positive. Species-specific sub-nodes may be required.
